title: Bitcoin institutional outflow reversal
what_changed: U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded over $1 billion in net outflows for the week ending May 15, 2026, breaking a 6-week streak of consecutive inflows that had totaled roughly $3.4 billion. BlackRock's IBIT alone saw a $136.25 million daily exit on May 15.
why_it_matters: The source brief describes this as the most significant weekly reversal of institutional capital since January 2026 and frames it as a macro-driven pullback as institutional investors reassess risk appetite amid rising Treasury yields and resistance near the $82,000 mark.
catalyst: Rising Treasury yields, uncertain Federal Reserve signals, and technical resistance at the 200-day moving average.
risks: Continued macro uncertainty could prolong ETF outflows.
Sustained high yields could weaken institutional support for BTC prices.
invalidation_conditions: Weekly net inflows into U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs resume.
IBIT or ARKB leads a renewed positive inflow cycle.
boundary: No trading execution, buy/sell instruction, or portfolio mutation. Research and monitoring only.
